# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

```ruby
class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    # Assuming that products have name, category and price attributes in their schema  
     @products = Product.all
      if params[:search] && !params[:search][:name].blank? || params[:search] && (params[:search][:price_range])         
        condition=[]  #Condition for AND or OR logic can be set according to your requirements here   e.g., 'AND', and more like that   
         unless params[:search][:category].nil?     if Rails.env.development?       then @products =@ products .where(["name LIKE ?", "%#{params[:search][:name]}%"]).or(@productstest)   else  @products= Products testes where(" name like '#{}' OR category_id #{'like', '%}')
      end         unless params.present?           condition << "1 = 1"             if !condition                 then product = Product .where(nil,*[params[:search].merge({:conditions=> conditions})])            else          pproduct=Products.. where("name like '#{}' OR category_id #{'like', '%'}", *[[paramst] merge(:conditons)
      end     render 'products/index' if condition.empty?         @filterrdefault = {:order=>"category asc"}          elsif params[:sort].present!           then begin            define  order= ActiveRecord::ConnectionAdapters :: AbstractAdapter adapter               "select set ..." ORDER BY '#{}')             else             
      end render 'products/index' if condition.empty?   @filterrdefault = {:order=>"category asc"}          elsif params[:sort].present!           then begin            define  order=ActiveRecord::ConnectionAdapters :: AbstractAdapter adapter               "select set ..."
